channel.title: Shaeel Afsar
channel.description: Personal professional website, portfolio, blog, and resume for Shaeel Afsar.

item.title: Designing the foundation for a calm, technical portfolio
item.description: A look at the design-system and architecture choices behind a portfolio that needs to feel cinematic, minimal, and maintainable.

item.title: Shipping server components without losing the human feel
item.description: How I keep Next.js pages server-first while still making them feel polished, interactive, and intentional.

item.title: Shipping interfaces with clarity instead of noise
item.description: A short essay on reducing visual noise, strengthening hierarchy, and helping products feel confident on first load.

item.title: Shipping calm interfaces in fast-moving product teams
item.description: Why restraint, structure, and interaction discipline usually create more confidence than louder UI ever can.

item.title: Making MDX work hard for technical writing
item.description: The content patterns I reach for when a blog post needs code, tables, callouts, and strong navigation without becoming a maintenance burden.

item.title: Building for speed and calm in modern Next.js projects
item.description: Notes on choosing Server Components by default, keeping client islands small, and making performance work visible to the team.

item.title: Content models that keep personal sites easy to evolve
item.description: A simple argument for structured frontmatter, reusable MDX utilities, and fewer one-off content decisions.
